skill.name: lead-research-assistant
skill.description: Helps sales and marketing teams identify qualified leads by analyzing a product, defining an ideal customer profile, researching matching companies, and suggesting outreach strategies.
skill.summary: Researches and prioritizes sales leads with fit scores and outreach guidance.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static external command and weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by markdown examples and ordinary prose, not executable code. The skill is safe to publish with a warning because its intended workflow may inspect a local codebase and perform external company research, which can expose sensitive business context if used carelessly.

medium_findings.title: External Lead Research Can Disclose Business Context
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to search for companies, review hiring and funding signals, and gather company context. This is legitimate for lead research, but users should avoid submitting confidential product plans, customer lists, or non-public sales strategy.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly directs external research for company matching and need signals. The behavior is intentional and useful, but it can expose sensitive business context through search queries.

medium_findings.title: Local Codebase Analysis May Expose Proprietary Details
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends running from a product source directory and analyzing the codebase to understand the product. This is useful for lead matching, but users should review what repository context may be shared with the assistant or external research workflow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly encourage repository analysis for product understanding. There is no exfiltration code, but proprietary source context could be included in prompts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at multiple markdown example blocks. These locations are fenced usage examples and output templates, not executable scripts or command invocations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown code fences containing natural-language prompts and an output format. No shell command, Ruby interpolation, or executable file is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Terms in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts correspond to ordinary product and outreach language, including description text and example content. No hashing, encryption, crypto library, or credential handling code appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a markdown instruction document with no code implementing cryptography. The flagged terms appear in normal business descriptions and examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert points to a field name in the suggested lead output format. The skill asks for business lead research, not port scanning, host discovery, or unauthorized network probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a section label for explaining why a company is a sales fit. It does not describe technical probing or network scanning.

content.value_statement: Sales teams often lose time researching poor-fit accounts and writing generic outreach. This skill turns product context and an ideal customer profile into prioritized leads with practical contact strategies.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes a product or service description to infer target customer needs.
Defines ideal customer profile criteria such as industry, size, location, and pain points.
Researches companies that match the requested lead criteria.
Scores and prioritizes leads based on fit, timing, budget signals, and relevance.
Suggests decision-maker roles, value propositions, conversation starters, and outreach strategies.
Can structure results for follow-up work such as CRM import or personalized outreach drafting.
limitations: Lead quality depends on the accuracy of user-provided product and customer criteria.
Public web data can be incomplete, outdated, or inconsistent across sources.
The skill does not guarantee valid contact details or sales conversion.
Users must follow privacy, consent, and anti-spam rules for any outreach.

use_cases.title: Build an Account List
use_cases.description: Create a ranked list of companies that match a defined product, market, location, and company size.

use_cases.title: Research Partnership Targets
use_cases.description: Find companies with complementary products and clear reasons to discuss a partnership.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Campaign Segment
use_cases.description: Turn an ideal customer profile into a focused prospect segment with messaging angles.

prompt_templates.title: Find Basic Leads
prompt_templates.prompt: I sell [product or service]. Find 10 companies in [industry or location] that could be strong leads. Include a fit score and a short reason for each.

prompt_templates.title: Use an Ideal Customer Profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this ideal customer profile: industry [industry], company size [range], location [region], pain points [list], current tools [tools]. Find 15 qualified leads and explain the outreach angle for each.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze My Product Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this repository to understand the product, then identify 10 companies that would benefit from it. Do not include sensitive implementation details in the final output.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize an Account Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Find 20 target accounts for [product]. Rank them by fit, timing, likely budget, and urgency. Include the decision-maker role, value proposition, and first outreach message angle.

output_examples.input: Find leads for a tool that protects sensitive data in AI coding assistant prompts.
output_examples.output: A prioritized list of fintech, healthcare, legal, and enterprise software companies that use AI coding tools. Each lead includes a fit score, reason for fit, target role, and privacy-focused outreach angle.

output_examples.input: Find Bay Area companies that need help with remote team productivity.
output_examples.output: A set of companies with remote hiring signals, distributed team context, likely productivity challenges, and suggested conversation starters for operations or people leaders.

output_examples.input: Create a CRM-ready prospect shortlist for a developer infrastructure product.
output: Company names and websites grouped by priority.
Fit explanations based on engineering signals and growth indicators.
Suggested buyer roles and first-message positioning for each account.

best_practices: Provide a clear product description, target market, and the problem your product solves.
Set firm constraints for industry, location, company size, and lead count.
Verify company details and outreach compliance before contacting prospects.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for scraped personal emails or private contact data.
Do not use vague prompts such as asking for any company that might buy.
Do not include confidential roadmap, customer, or source code details unless needed.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide your product description, target customer, location or industry constraints, and the number of leads you need.

faq.question: Can it analyze my codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can use repository context to infer the product, but you should avoid exposing confidential details unnecessarily.

faq.question: Does it provide verified contact details?
faq.answer: No. It suggests target roles and outreach strategies. You should verify contacts through approved sales tools or public sources.

faq.question: Can it create outreach messages?
faq.answer: Yes. After lead research, it can draft personalized outreach angles and message starters for each account.

faq.question: Is this useful for non-sales teams?
faq.answer: Yes. Founders, marketers, partnership teams, and consultants can use it to identify relevant organizations.

faq.question: What should I check before using the results?
faq.answer: Confirm company facts, respect privacy rules, and make sure outreach follows applicable email and platform policies.
